Description:
Set up the job-discovery monorepo root with Docker multi-stage build, Nginx reverse proxy, Supervisor process manager, and local docker-compose orchestration.

SCOPE:
- Monorepo initialisation with AGENT.md index, .env.example, .gitignore
- Multi-stage Dockerfile: node:22-alpine frontend build stage into python:3.14-slim runtime stage
- Nginx reverse proxy: / → static export, /api/v1/* → FastAPI, /health passthrough
- Supervisor config: migrate (priority 1) → nginx (10) → fastapi (10)
- docker-compose.yml for local development with healthcheck

MILESTONE: MVP 1 — Scraper Foundation & In-Memory Store

Create the job-discovery/ root directory and foundational files for the monorepo.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create job-discovery/ root directory
- Create root AGENT.md as index file listing all sub-agent files and their responsibilities
- Create .env.example with all required environment variable keys (no values)
- Create .gitignore covering Python (__pycache__, .venv, *.pyc), Node (node_modules, .next, out/), Docker, and secrets (.env)

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: None

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Directory structure matches proposal-v4-structure.md
- AGENT.md index references all downstream AGENT.md files
- .env.example contains all keys required by settings.py
- .gitignore prevents secrets and build artefacts from being committed

EDGE CASES:
- Ensure .env is listed in .gitignore before any secrets are added

Create the production Dockerfile using a multi-stage build strategy.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Stage 1 (builder): node:22-alpine; install pnpm; copy frontend/; run pnpm install and pnpm build (output: export)
- Stage 2 (runtime): python:3.14-slim; install uv; copy backend/ and built frontend static files from stage 1
- Install nginx and supervisor in runtime stage
- Copy nginx.conf and supervisord.conf into runtime image
- Expose port 80
- ENTRYPOINT: supervisord

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- docker build -t job-discovery . completes without errors
- docker run serves frontend on port 80
- /api/v1/ routes proxied to FastAPI correctly
- /health endpoint returns 200

EDGE CASES:
- Layer caching: copy pyproject.toml before source to cache pip install layer
- Ensure node_modules not copied into runtime stage

Create supervisord.conf to manage process startup order inside the container.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- [program:migrate]: runs Alembic or DB init script; priority=1; startsecs=0; autorestart=false
- [program:nginx]: priority=10; autorestart=true
- [program:fastapi]: uvicorn backend.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000; priority=10; autorestart=true
- stdout and stderr logs routed to /dev/stdout and /dev/stderr for Docker log capture
- [supervisord] nodaemon=true

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- supervisord starts all three programs in correct priority order
- FastAPI available on port 8000 internally
- Nginx available on port 80
- Logs visible via docker logs

EDGE CASES:
- migrate program must complete before fastapi starts; use depends_on or priority gap

Create nginx.conf to route traffic between the static frontend and FastAPI backend.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- server block listening on port 80
- location /: root pointing to Next.js static export directory; try_files with index.html fallback for SPA routing
- location /api/v1/: proxy_pass to http://127.0.0.1:8000; set proxy headers (Host, X-Real-IP, X-Forwarded-For)
- location /health: proxy_pass to FastAPI /health; no caching
- gzip compression enabled for JS, CSS, JSON
- Cache-Control headers for static assets

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- / serves Next.js index.html
- /api/v1/jobs returns JSON from FastAPI
- /health returns 200
- SPA deep links (e.g. /jobs/123) resolve to index.html

EDGE CASES:
- Trailing slash normalisation for API routes
- CORS headers not duplicated between Nginx and FastAPI

Create docker-compose.yml for local development orchestration.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Single service: job-discovery; build: .; ports: 8080:80
- env_file: .env
- volumes: mount backend/ and frontend/ for hot-reload during development (optional override)
- healthcheck: GET http://localhost/health every 30s; retries: 3
- restart: unless-stopped

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- docker compose up builds and starts successfully
- Application accessible at http://localhost:8080
- healthcheck passes within 90s of start
- docker compose down stops all services cleanly

EDGE CASES:
- Port conflicts on 8080; document alternative port override via .env
- Volume mounts may conflict with Docker-internal build artefacts

Implement the FastAPI application foundation including typed settings, structured JSON logging, domain models, keyword filtering, and an in-memory fake database for MVP 1.

SCOPE:
- FastAPI entrypoint with /api/v1/ prefix and agent auto-discovery
- Pydantic Settings for all environment variables
- Structured JSON logger shared across all modules
- Domain models: Job and ScrapeResult
- Keyword filter for title/description matching
- In-memory dict-backed fake DB (MVP 1 only; replaced by Supabase in MVP 2)

MILESTONE: MVP 1 — Scraper Foundation & In-Memory Store

Create backend/main.py as the FastAPI application entrypoint.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Instantiate FastAPI app with title, version, and /api/v1 prefix on all routers
- Import and include routers: scrape.py and jobs.py
- Agent auto-discovery: import all agents from agents/registry.py on startup
- Lifespan context manager for startup/shutdown events
- In-memory fake DB: module-level dict jobs_store: dict[str, Job] = {}
- Add comment: # MVP 1 EXCEPTION: replace with Supabase asyncpg pool in Epic 8
- /health endpoint returning {status": "ok"

Issue id: - Circular import between main.py and routers; use deferred imports if needed"

Description:
Create backend/settings.py with typed and validated environment variable configuration.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Use pydantic-settings BaseSettings class
- Fields: APP_ENV (str, default dev), LOG_LEVEL (str, default INFO), SCRAPE_CONCURRENCY (int, default 3), KEYWORD_FILTER_LIST (list[str], default [])
- MVP 2 stubs (Optional): DATABASE_URL, OPENAI_API_KEY, AZURE_STORAGE_URL
- Settings loaded from .env via model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file='.env')
- Singleton: settings = Settings() at module level; imported by all modules
- Validation: APP_ENV must be one of dev/staging/prod

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Settings loads correctly from .env.example values
- Invalid APP_ENV raises ValidationError at startup
- Missing required fields raise clear error messages
- tsc --noEmit equivalent: uv run python -c 'from backend.settings import settings'

EDGE CASES:
- .env file missing in production container; all required fields must have defaults or fail fast with clear message

Create backend/logging_config.py providing a shared structured JSON logger.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- get_logger(name: str) -> logging.Logger factory function
- JSON formatter: outputs {timestamp, level, logger, message, **extra} per line
- Log level driven by settings.LOG_LEVEL
- All log output to stdout (Docker-compatible)
- Usage: logger = get_logger(__name__) in every module
- Create backend/AGENT.md documenting logger usage convention

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Log output is valid JSON parseable by jq
- Log level filtering works (DEBUG suppressed in INFO mode)
- Logger name appears in every log line
- Extra fields passed to logger.info('msg', extra={...}) appear in JSON output

EDGE CASES:
- Avoid double-logging when uvicorn access logs are also enabled; configure propagate=False

Create backend/models.py with Pydantic schemas for core domain entities.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Job model: id (UUID, auto), title (str), company (str), location (str), description (str), source (str), url (HttpUrl), salary_min (Optional[int]), salary_max (Optional[int]), relevance_score (float, default 0.0), scraped_at (datetime, auto)
- ScrapeResult model: source_id (str), jobs_found (int), jobs_new (int), duration_seconds (float), errors (list[str], default [])
- All models use model_config = ConfigDict(str_strip_whitespace=True)
- Export __all__ listing both models

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Job model rejects missing required fields
- HttpUrl validates malformed URLs
- UUID auto-generated when id not provided
- ScrapeResult serialises cleanly to JSON

EDGE CASES:
- salary fields optional to support roles without published salary
- relevance_score validated to range 0.0–1.0 using field_validator

Create backend/filters.py implementing title and description keyword filtering.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- filter_jobs(jobs: list[Job], keywords: list[str]) -> list[Job]
- Case-insensitive substring matching against job.title and job.description
- If keywords list is empty, return all jobs unfiltered
- Matching logic: job passes if ANY keyword matches title OR description
- Keyword list sourced from settings.KEYWORD_FILTER_LIST at runtime

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2-4

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Empty keyword list returns all jobs
- Single keyword match on title returns correct jobs
- Single keyword match on description returns correct jobs
- Case-insensitive: 'Python' matches 'python developer'
- No match returns empty list

EDGE CASES:
- Keywords with special regex characters must not cause errors (use plain substring, not regex)
- Very large keyword lists: consider set-based lookup optimisation

Wire the in-memory dict-backed job store into main.py as an MVP 1 exception.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Module-level: jobs_store: dict[str, Job] = {} in backend/main.py
- Helper functions: add_jobs(jobs: list[Job]) -> None (deduplicates by URL), get_jobs(skip: int, limit: int) -> list[Job], get_job(job_id: str) -> Optional[Job]
- Deduplication: skip jobs whose URL already exists in store
- Add prominent comment block: # MVP 1 EXCEPTION — in-memory store; replaced by asyncpg pool in Epic 8 (MVP 2)

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2-4

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Duplicate URLs not inserted twice
- get_jobs returns correct page slice
- get_job returns None for unknown IDs
- Store persists across requests within same process lifetime

EDGE CASES:
- Thread safety: FastAPI async workers share same process; dict access is GIL-protected but document the limitation

Build the scraper plugin system including BaseScrapeAgent abstract class, @register decorator registry, LinkedIn and JobServe Playwright scrapers, and the /api/v1/scrape POST endpoint.

SCOPE:
- BaseScrapeAgent ABC defining source_id, display_name, run() interface
- Registry with @register decorator, get_all_agents(), get_agent(source_id)
- LinkedIn scraper: anti-bot randomisation, deduplication
- JobServe scraper: pagination randomisation, data normalisation
- POST /api/v1/scrape router: calls get_all_agents() — no hardcoded source names

MILESTONE: MVP 1 — Scraper Foundation & In-Memory Store

Create backend/agents/base.py defining the abstract base class for all scraper agents.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- BaseScrapeAgent(ABC):
  - Abstract property source_id: str
  - Abstract property display_name: str
  - Abstract async method run(keywords: list[str]) -> ScrapeResult
- Import Job and ScrapeResult from backend.models
- Docstring explaining the contract each agent must fulfil
- Create backend/agents/__init__.py (empty, enables package import)

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2-4

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Instantiating BaseScrapeAgent directly raises TypeError
- Subclass missing run() raises TypeError on instantiation
- Subclass implementing all abstract members instantiates correctly
- source_id and display_name accessible as properties

EDGE CASES:
- run() must be async to support Playwright and httpx; enforce with abstractmethod + async def

Create backend/agents/registry.py with the @register decorator and agent lookup functions.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- _REGISTRY: dict[str, type[BaseScrapeAgent]] = {} module-level store
- @register decorator: registers decorated class by its source_id; raises ValueError on duplicate source_id
- get_all_agents() -> list[BaseScrapeAgent]: instantiates and returns all registered agents
- get_agent(source_id: str) -> BaseScrapeAgent: returns single agent or raises KeyError
- Create backend/agents/AGENT.md documenting how to add a new scraper (copy template, apply @register, implement run())

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-3-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- @register adds agent to _REGISTRY under correct source_id
- Duplicate source_id raises ValueError
- get_all_agents() returns instances of all registered agents
- get_agent('linkedin') returns LinkedIn agent instance
- get_agent('unknown') raises KeyError

EDGE CASES:
- Registry populated at import time; ensure all agent modules are imported before registry is queried (handled in main.py startup)

Implement backend/agents/linkedin/linkedin_agent.py as a Playwright-based scraper.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Class LinkedInAgent(BaseScrapeAgent) decorated with @register
- source_id = 'linkedin'; display_name = 'LinkedIn'
- run(keywords: list[str]) -> ScrapeResult:
  - Launch Playwright chromium in headless mode
  - Construct LinkedIn jobs search URL with keyword query params
  - Anti-bot: randomised viewport size, user-agent rotation, randomised scroll delay (0.5–2.5s)
  - Extract: title, company, location, description, url from job listing cards
  - Deduplication: skip jobs whose URL already exists in jobs_store
  - Return ScrapeResult with counts and any errors caught per listing
- Create backend/agents/linkedin/AGENT.md

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-3-2, JD-2-6

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Agent registers under source_id 'linkedin'
- run() returns ScrapeResult with jobs_found >= 0
- Extracted Job objects pass Pydantic validation
- Deduplication skips already-stored URLs
- Playwright errors caught and added to ScrapeResult.errors list

EDGE CASES:
- LinkedIn may block headless browsers; document fallback to playwright-stealth or proxy rotation
- Rate limiting: add configurable delay between page requests
- Missing fields on individual listings must not crash entire run

Implement backend/agents/jobserve/jobserve_agent.py as a Playwright-based scraper.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Class JobServeAgent(BaseScrapeAgent) decorated with @register
- source_id = 'jobserve'; display_name = 'JobServe'
- run(keywords: list[str]) -> ScrapeResult:
  - Launch Playwright chromium in headless mode
  - Navigate to JobServe search with keyword params
  - Pagination: iterate through result pages with randomised delay (1–3s) between pages
  - Data normalisation: map JobServe-specific field names to Job schema (title, company, location, description, url)
  - Salary extraction: parse salary strings into salary_min/salary_max integers where present
  - Deduplication against jobs_store
  - Return ScrapeResult
- Create backend/agents/jobserve/AGENT.md

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-3-2, JD-2-6

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Agent registers under source_id 'jobserve'
- Pagination navigates multiple pages correctly
- Salary strings parsed correctly (e.g. '£50,000 - £60,000' -> salary_min=50000, salary_max=60000)
- Normalised Job objects pass Pydantic validation
- Per-listing errors do not abort entire scrape run

EDGE CASES:
- JobServe pagination may use JavaScript rendering; ensure Playwright waits for network idle
- Some listings may lack salary; salary fields remain None
- URL deduplication must use normalised canonical URLs

Create backend/routers/scrape.py implementing POST /api/v1/scrape.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Router: APIRouter(prefix='/api/v1', tags=['scrape'])
- POST /scrape endpoint:
  - Accept optional ScrapeRequest body: {keywords: list[str]}
  - Call get_all_agents() — never hardcode source names
  - Run all agents concurrently with asyncio.gather(*[agent.run(keywords) for agent in agents])
  - Pass scraped jobs to add_jobs() in fake DB (MVP 1)
  - Return list[ScrapeResult]
- Include router in main.py
- Log start, per-agent completion, and any errors using get_logger(__name__)

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-3-2, JD-2-6

TESTING CRITERIA:
- POST /api/v1/scrape returns 200 with list of ScrapeResult objects
- Endpoint calls all registered agents (not hardcoded list)
- Concurrent execution: both agents run in parallel
- Failed agent does not abort other agents
- Keywords forwarded correctly to each agent

EDGE CASES:
- Empty agent registry returns empty list gracefully
- asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True to prevent one agent failure cancelling others
